tasks.criterion: planner.md declares adaptive-thinking frontmatter at effort medium: agents/planner.md's YAML frontmatter contains a thinking key with type: adaptive and effort: medium.
tasks.verification_command: grep -q '^thinking:' agents/planner.md && grep -qE 'type:[[:space:]]*adaptive' agents/planner.md && grep -qE 'effort:[[:space:]]*medium' agents/planner.md

tasks.criterion: generator.md declares adaptive-thinking frontmatter at effort medium: agents/generator.md's YAML frontmatter contains a thinking key with type: adaptive and effort: medium. This is the IMPLEMENTATION-mode baseline; per-mode overrides for CONTRACT_PROPOSAL and CONTRACT_REVISION are documented in the body.
tasks.verification_command: grep -q '^thinking:' agents/generator.md && grep -qE 'type:[[:space:]]*adaptive' agents/generator.md && grep -qE 'effort:[[:space:]]*medium' agents/generator.md

tasks.criterion: evaluator.md declares adaptive-thinking frontmatter at effort high: agents/evaluator.md's YAML frontmatter contains a thinking key with type: adaptive and effort: high. high is the capability-eval baseline; the body documents medium for regression-criterion evaluation (Sprint 7 wiring point) and max for CONTRACT_REVIEW.
tasks.verification_command: grep -q '^thinking:' agents/evaluator.md && grep -qE 'type:[[:space:]]*adaptive' agents/evaluator.md && grep -qE 'effort:[[:space:]]*high' agents/evaluator.md

tasks.criterion: harness-summary SKILL declares adaptive-thinking frontmatter at effort max: skills/harness-summary/SKILL.md's YAML frontmatter contains a thinking key with type: adaptive and effort: max. Summary work is analysis-heavy (cross-sprint trends, saturation, ACI review) and benefits from max effort.
tasks.verification_command: grep -q '^thinking:' skills/harness-summary/SKILL.md && grep -qE 'type:[[:space:]]*adaptive' skills/harness-summary/SKILL.md && grep -qE 'effort:[[:space:]]*max' skills/harness-summary/SKILL.md

tasks.criterion: config.json schema extended with thinking.profile: .harness/config.json contains a thinking object with a profile key whose value is one of "default", "fast", or "thorough".
config.json schema extended with batch fields: .harness/config.json contains a batch object with keys enabled (boolean) and min_criteria (integer). Defaults: enabled: false and min_criteria: 20.
tasks.verification_command: jq -e '.batch | has("enabled") and has("min_criteria") and (.enabled | type == "boolean") and (.min_criteria | type == "number")' .harness/config.json

tasks.criterion: harness-sprint SKILL contains a Batch API section: skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md contains a Batch section referencing batch.enabled, batch.min_criteria, the guard condition, the submit/poll/map-back workflow, the 50% discount and 24-hour SLA, and the synchronous fallback.
tasks.verification_command: grep -qiE '^## .*Batch|^### .*Batch' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md && grep -q 'batch.enabled' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md && grep -q 'batch.min_criteria' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md && grep -q '50%' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md && grep -qE '24[- ]hour' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md

tasks.criterion: README.md documents the new config knobs: README.md contains documentation for thinking.profile (mentioning the three values default / fast / thorough), batch.enabled, and batch.min_criteria.
tasks.verification_command: grep -q 'thinking.profile' README.md && grep -q 'default' README.md && grep -q 'fast' README.md && grep -q 'thorough' README.md && grep -q 'batch.enabled' README.md && grep -q 'batch.min_criteria' README.md

tasks.criterion: sprint-08.tasks.json is emitted: .harness/contracts/sprint-08.tasks.json exists, is valid JSON, has a top-level sprint: 8 field and a tasks array with one entry per criterion in this contract (including gate criteria with is_gate: true, weight: 0).
tasks.verification_command: jq -e '.sprint == 8 and (.tasks | length) >= 13 and (.tasks | all(has("task_id") and has("grader_type") and has("weight") and has("is_gate")))' .harness/contracts/sprint-08.tasks.json

tasks.criterion: Per-mode thinking overrides are internally consistent: A reader of agents/planner.md, agents/generator.md, agents/evaluator.md, and skills/harness-summary/SKILL.md can explain the frontmatter baseline for each (planner/generator: medium, evaluator: high, summary: max), the per-mode overrides in each agent's body (Generator CONTRACT_PROPOSAL/REVISION > IMPLEMENTATION; Evaluator CONTRACT_REVIEW at max, capability EVALUATION at high, regression-criterion evaluation at medium), and that the Sprint-7 policy-only caveat is updated to reflect frontmatter wiring.

Backward compatibility is explicit and correct: A pre-Phase-2 project with no thinking object and no batch object in config.json runs the harness identically to Phase 1. Each new config field has a documented default. Adaptive-thinking frontmatter is advisory — if the runtime ignores it, agents still run with prior model-default behavior.

Batch threshold rationale is justified, not just asserted: The Batch section in skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md explains why batch.enabled: false is the default (24-hour SLA unsuitable for tight iteration) and why batch.min_criteria: 20 is the threshold (break-even point where 50% discount compounds; small sprints pay more in latency than they save).

thinking.profile override semantics are clear: The documentation explains how default / fast / thorough map to effort vectors across the four agents/skills (fast = shift down clamped at medium; thorough = shift up clamped at max), how to set the profile in config.json, and how per-mode overrides compose on top of the profile-adjusted baseline.

No silent behavior change for pre-Phase-2 configs: A project whose .harness/config.json lacks both the thinking object and the batch object must run the harness identically to Phase 1 — no batch submission, no missing-key errors, every agent spawn proceeds with pre-Sprint-8 semantics.
No modification of prior sprint artifacts: .harness/evals/sprint-0[1-7]* and .harness/contracts/sprint-0[1-7]* must be unchanged from HEAD at the start of this sprint (append-only rule).

Sprint-7 evaluator thinking-effort policy is preserved, not contradicted: Regression at medium, capability at high, contract review at max — the Sprint-8 frontmatter and body must not introduce contradicting values.
